canonicality: Bootstrap view; protected transactional state becomes canonical when implemented.

stage_5_completion_candidate.note: Nilhan approved Stage 5 complete, but no release blocker is closed by that stage decision. Stage 6 protected authentication, evidence custody, sandbox enforcement and later release/evaluation gates remain binding.

blockers.title: Unsupported trust boundary
blockers.criterion: the deployment/enforcement contract, trusted computing base, or support matrix is missing or contradicted by implementation.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.

blockers.title: Bypassable mediation
blockers.criterion: a claimed filesystem, command, network, browser, secret, signing, evidence, review, publication, or side-effect permission can bypass its enforcement point.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.

blockers.title: Architect direct authority
blockers.criterion: a model process can directly access signing keys, privileged state transitions, non-waivable overrides, or release authority.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.
blockers.stage_5_completion_note: Purpose-separated private keys remained inside the dedicated Docker volume during the passing candidate. The supported row still trusts Nilhan and the Windows/Docker administrator; RB-003 remains OPEN for later integrated authority review.

blockers.title: Private-pack leakage
blockers.criterion: seeded master LAOS, future actions, hidden tests, private evaluator answers, Architect notes, secrets, or personal data appear in an execution deliverable, or pack construction is not an allowlisted projection.

blockers.title: Early or replayed authority
blockers.criterion: in a connected privileged support row, a weaker agent can obtain the next action early, redeem a capsule twice, use authority outside the measured revocation bound or after expiry, or use it under the wrong actor, audience, project, base seal, state, policy, profile, or key purpose; or offline v8.0 permits any privileged redemption/dispatch.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Protected-envelope v2 authenticates authority and time context, trust keys are issuer-bound, pack context must agree, and the local protected signer candidate passed. This blocker remains OPEN after Nilhan Stage 5 approval; integrated release gates remain.

blockers.title: Malformed canonical state
blockers.criterion: a malformed, duplicate-key, oversized, unknown-version, semantically invalid, or unauthenticated trusted record can enter canonical state.
blockers.stage_2_note: Typed parsing and validation are implemented, but the blocker remains open until protected canonical-state integration and independent verification exist.

blockers.title: Repository identity ambiguity
blockers.criterion: capture/continuation fingerprints disagree, pre-claim drift can be hidden, cross-platform golden vectors disagree, or base/result lineage is incomplete.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.
blockers.stage_5_completion_note: The sealed-v7 capture reproduced identical source seals before and after, bound six evidence areas, and issued an unredeemed first capsule. RB-007 remains OPEN until later integrated lineage and release gates close.

blockers.title: Unsafe promotion
blockers.criterion: the builder can mutate the authoritative checkout directly, stale-base promotion succeeds, or filesystem/database crashes can leave an accepted result ambiguous.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.

blockers.title: Filesystem/archive escape
blockers.criterion: a supported-platform path, archive, symlink, junction, reparse, hard-link, alias, or concurrent-swap escape remains possible.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Template paths now deny Unicode/case aliases, Windows trailing-dot/space ambiguity, control paths, and case collisions; writes and Git genesis are hardened. This blocker remains OPEN for the complete supported-platform escape and concurrency corpus.

blockers.title: State race or fork
blockers.criterion: two workers can claim/advance the same action, state and audit event can diverge, or rollback/fork/clock anomalies can silently change authority.
blockers.stage_3_note: Local Security Spine controls are exercised, but this blocker remains open pending independent review and later integrated release gates.

blockers.title: False independence
blockers.criterion: typed actor names or shared principals, credentials, contexts, workspaces, or controlling authority can impersonate independent roles or quorum.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Ordinary JSON naming Nilhan is explicitly unauthenticated and cannot produce approval. This blocker remains OPEN until Stage 6 supplies protected Nilhan identity and independent review custody.

blockers.title: Unsupported closure
blockers.criterion: a mandatory criterion can close without current result-seal-bound evidence and required independent review, or the builder can alter protected/hidden tests.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Current Stage 3 evidence now requires a clean source, regeneration by fixed collectors, run/source binding, and a 15-minute freshness window. Stage 5 receipts require exact command arrays and exact retained artifact hashes, but truthfully declare that the builder is unauthenticated. This blocker remains OPEN until protected evidence custody and owner-stage review exist.

blockers.title: Unsafe execution
blockers.criterion: arbitrary repository code or a dangerous command can run through unrestricted host access, or high-risk work silently proceeds without a conformance-tested sandbox.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: The Stage 5 candidate builder is local developer/operator tooling and runs its fixed verification commands on that host. The focused scan did not establish a lower-privilege attack path, but RB-013 remains OPEN for Stage 6 protected execution and no product sandbox claim is made.

blockers.title: Unsafe egress, model transfer, or secrets
blockers.criterion: network/browser/model-provider policy can be bypassed, cloud metadata or disallowed targets are reachable, built-in provider tools escape mediation, or credentials/private data leak through prompts, provider retention, processes, evidence, artifacts, or allowed channels.

blockers.title: Unsafe side effects
blockers.criterion: external effects can bypass approval, idempotency, reconciliation, or independent verification; an indeterminate irreversible operation can retry automatically; or unauthorized verification raises the wrong error or advances state.

blockers.title: Mutable release contamination
blockers.criterion: SQLite, WAL/SHM, locks, logs, leases, caches, current indexes, secrets, or other live runtime state appears in an immutable release or static content manifest.

blockers.title: Evidence/audit weakness
blockers.criterion: evidence mutation passes, canonical evidence is builder-writable, critical audit truncation/fork/rollback passes, or required external anchoring is absent.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Run-bound atomic receipts prevent stale PASS reuse, regenerate current Stage 3 evidence, enforce exact commands, and hash retained candidate artifacts. They explicitly remain builder-asserted bootstrap evidence in the repository. This blocker remains OPEN until broker-controlled custody, authenticated producers, immutable anchoring, and the owner-stage audit controls exist.

blockers.title: Artifact/provenance failure
blockers.criterion: ZIP/wheel extraction, installation, full retest, SBOM, provenance, signature identity/predicate verification, reproducibility claim, consumer policy, rollback protection, or durable retrieval fails.
blockers.stage_5_security_note: Ephemeral source and wheel outputs are now represented only by the exact uv build command gate and are not claimed as retained candidate artifacts. This blocker remains OPEN for Stage 8 release artifact custody, SBOM, provenance, reproducibility, consumer policy, and durable retrieval.

blockers.title: Broken operator contract
blockers.criterion: required documentation points to nonexistent files, a supported clean install cannot complete operator journeys, or capability claims exceed the support matrix.

blockers.title: Migration failure
blockers.criterion: supported v7 import, dry run, mapping report, idempotent rerun, rollback/coexistence, or migration rehearsal fails.

blockers.title: Unrecoverable trust compromise
blockers.criterion: kill switch, revocation, quarantine, key/secret rotation, external recovery point, revalidation, and re-attestation drills do not recover safely.

blockers.title: Critical or uncontrolled High security finding
blockers.criterion: a Critical finding is open, or a High finding lacks independent security review plus release-authority and human-quorum acceptance. Critical is non-waivable. An unaccepted High disables every affected support row. Any accepted High or lower-severity risk is signed and bound to exact code/environment/evidence digests, with named ownership, scope, compensating controls, expiry, and revalidation trigger.

blockers.title: Invalid or missing evaluation
blockers.criterion: the preregistered sealed evaluation has not completed, has unresolved protocol violations, or its materials/results cannot be independently audited without exposing active hidden content.

blockers.title: v8.0 efficacy/safety floor not met
blockers.criterion: the predefined v8.0 minimum efficacy, safety, non-inferiority, and resource thresholds are not met. An inconclusive or negative system may remain a technical preview but cannot be declared v8.0 GA or claim improved weaker-agent performance.

blockers.title: Missing final artifacts
blockers.criterion: a claimed final deliverable is absent from durable storage or has not been retrieved, reopened, hashed, safely extracted where applicable, and independently verified after final transfer.
